512	### 8.3 The failure is behavioral, and it is specific
513	
514	Among the 136 `never_fired` anchors, **136/136 (100%) still emit a `[STATE: X → Y]` marker** and
515	only 4/136 name the expected tool in prose. The model reliably performs the state-annotation half
516	of the turn and substitutes a **clarifying question to the user** for the tool call. In free-running
517	T1 the model reproduced the announce style — narration, no call — on **178/200 (89%)** of anchors.
518	
519	Representative `never_fired` anchor (`L3_059_8`, gold tool `qualify_lead`); T2 transitions correctly,
520	then asks the user for exactly the fields the tool takes:
521	
522	> `[STATE: GREETING → QUALIFY_PROSPECT]`
523	> …เพื่อที่จะแนะนำแพ็กเกจที่เหมาะสมกับคุณที่สุด ขออนุญาตสอบถามข้อมูลเบื้องต้น… ไม่ทราบว่าคุณชื่ออะไร
524	> และติดต่อจากบริษัทไหนคะ รวมถึงงบประมาณที่วางไว้และกรอบเวลาที่อยากเริ่มใช้งาน…
525	
526	### 8.4 What this does and does not overturn
527	
528	**Falsified:** §7.3's hypothesis that the model "fires the call on its own next turn once a user
529	reply arrives, and the single-turn eval just can't see it." It does not — not even under a
530	two-turn window and a more generous success criterion. §7.1's row-slicing finding remains factually
531	correct about the *corpus* (the teacher does split narrate-then-call across two turns), but that
532	structure does **not** rescue the *policy*: the model has internalized the announce half and
533	under-emits the call half regardless of turn granularity. The priority does **not** shift to
534	"fix the single-turn eval" on the strength of this probe.
535	
536	**Not reproduced, and worth flagging:** the §1 headline **0.087** tool-F1 does not appear on this
537	slice — condition A, the single-turn metric, scores **0.258** mean strict tool-F1 and a 0.335
538	emission rate on 200 bare-call anchors. The gap is real but **materially smaller than the headline
539	number implies**. Candidate explanations, none yet tested: the §1 figure came from n=52 rows on the
540	*pre*-regeneration corpus and mixed bare-call with fused targets, whereas this is n=200 bare-call-only
541	anchors on the regenerated corpus. **The 0.087 figure should not be quoted again until re-measured
542	on current data.**
543	
544	### 8.5 Caveats
545	
546	- **Scope:** only the 381 sliceable anchors were probed; the 414 whose announce follows a `tool`
547	  response were excluded to keep A and B on matching prompt shapes. The result generalizes to
548	  roughly half the bare-call anchor population.
549	- **Counterfactual continuation:** B's round-2 user message is the gold reply to the *gold*
550	  announce. For the 178/200 anchors where the model's T1 was its own announce, that reply is
551	  approximately-valid rather than exact. Removing this needs a user simulator. Note the caveat
552	  cuts *toward* B — a better-matched reply could only help B, and B still lost.
553	- **Greedy only.** No pass@k. A sampled probe could still find the call in the distribution's
554	  tail; that is the outstanding pass@k item from §4 step 2, not addressed here.
555	- **§7.3's finish_reason/truncation check remains unrun.** It was not needed to reach this
556	  verdict — 100% of never-fired completions terminate with coherent prose well inside the
557	  512-token budget, which is not the signature of truncation — but it is still formally open.
558	
559	### 8.6 Net standing
560	
561	§4 step 1's forensics are now complete enough to act on. The gap survives the eval-granularity
562	confound, so the §7.3 off-ramp ("fix the eval, not the policy") is closed. Standing position is
563	unchanged from §6's fork and now better-evidenced: SFT-only composite 0.7167 (FAIL vs 0.75),
564	RFT headroom MARGINAL, GRPO revival dead, and the tool-emission weakness confirmed as policy
565	behaviour. **Next move per §4 step 4:** the combined factorial run (`response_only` masking +
566	tool-turn upsampling) on sanitized data, with the target bar re-derived for the per-turn-fair
567	metric first — plus the still-outstanding pass@k probe, which is now the cheapest remaining way
568	to learn whether the call exists in the sampling distribution at all before committing to a retrain.
569	
570	---

file.content:
**delta = −0.015** (material bar: ≥ +0.20) → **VERDICT: POLICY_DEFECT.**

Free-running B outcomes: `fired_at_t1` 11.0% (22) — the model fused the call into its own
announce turn, *earlier* than gold; `fired_at_t2` 21.0% (42); **`never_fired` 68.0% (136)**.

The paired churn is **noise, not a wash of two real effects**: 17 anchors recovered by
free-running, 20 lost, exact two-sided McNemar on the 37 discordant pairs **p = 0.743**. Only
12.8% of A's failures are recovered by B, and B loses about as many. Per level, the delta never
points consistently one way (L3 +0.072, L4 −0.067, L5 −0.087).

### 8.3 The failure is behavioral, and it is specific

Among the 136 `never_fired` anchors, **136/136 (100%) still emit a `[STATE: X → Y]` marker** and
only 4/136 name the expected tool in prose. The model reliably performs the state-annotation half
of the turn and substitutes a **clarifying question to the user** for the tool call. In free-running
T1 the model reproduced the announce style — narration, no call — on **178/200 (89%)** of anchors.

Representative `never_fired` anchor (`L3_059_8`, gold tool `qualify_lead`); T2 transitions correctly,
then asks the user for exactly the fields the tool takes:

> `[STATE: GREETING → QUALIFY_PROSPECT]`
> …เพื่อที่จะแนะนำแพ็กเกจที่เหมาะสมกับคุณที่สุด ขออนุญาตสอบถามข้อมูลเบื้องต้น… ไม่ทราบว่าคุณชื่ออะไร
> และติดต่อจากบริษัทไหนคะ รวมถึงงบประมาณที่วางไว้และกรอบเวลาที่อยากเริ่มใช้งาน…

### 8.4 What this does and does not overturn

**Falsified:** §7.3's hypothesis that the model "fires the call on its own next turn once a user
reply arrives, and the single-turn eval just can't see it." It does not — not even under a
two-turn window and a more generous success criterion. §7.1's row-slicing finding remains factually
correct about the *corpus* (the teacher does split narrate-then-call across two turns), but that
structure does **not** rescue the *policy*: the model has internalized the announce half and
under-emits the call half regardless of turn granularity. The priority does **not** shift to
"fix the single-turn eval" on the strength of this probe.

**Not reproduced, and worth flagging:** the §1 headline **0.087** tool-F1 does not appear on this
slice — condition A, the single-turn metric, scores **0.258** mean strict tool-F1 and a 0.335
emission rate on 200 bare-call anchors. The gap is real but **materially smaller than the headline
number implies**. Candidate explanations, none yet tested: the §1 figure came from n=52 rows on the
*pre*-regeneration corpus and mixed bare-call with fused targets, whereas this is n=200 bare-call-only
anchors on the regenerated corpus. **The 0.087 figure should not be quoted again until re-measured
on current data.**

### 8.5 Caveats

- **Scope:** only the 381 sliceable anchors were probed; the 414 whose announce follows a `tool`
  response were excluded to keep A and B on matching prompt shapes. The result generalizes to
  roughly half the bare-call anchor population.
- **Counterfactual continuation:** B's round-2 user message is the gold reply to the *gold*
  announce. For the 178/200 anchors where the model's T1 was its own announce, that reply is
  approximately-valid rather than exact. Removing this needs a user simulator. Note the caveat
  cuts *toward* B — a better-matched reply could only help B, and B still lost.
- **Greedy only.** No pass@k. A sampled probe could still find the call in the distribution's
  tail; that is the outstanding pass@k item from §4 step 2, not addressed here.
- **§7.3's finish_reason/truncation check remains unrun.** It was not needed to reach this
  verdict — 100% of never-fired completions terminate with coherent prose well inside the
  512-token budget, which is not the signature of truncation — but it is still formally open.

### 8.6 Net standing

§4 step 1's forensics are now complete enough to act on. The gap survives the eval-granularity
confound, so the §7.3 off-ramp ("fix the eval, not the policy") is closed. Standing position is
unchanged from §6's fork and now better-evidenced: SFT-only composite 0.7167 (FAIL vs 0.75),
RFT headroom MARGINAL, GRPO revival dead, and the tool-emission weakness confirmed as policy
behaviour. **Next move per §4 step 4:** the combined factorial run (`response_only` masking +
tool-turn upsampling) on sanitized data, with the target bar re-derived for the per-turn-fair
metric first — plus the still-outstanding pass@k probe, which is now the cheapest remaining way
to learn whether the call exists in the sampling distribution at all before committing to a retrain.

# Findings: state-transition accuracy drop (0.817 → 0.687) — data-side vs model-side

## 1. The audit script

**Primary:** `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py` (the row-level audit that produced both the n=150 0.817 and the n=284 0.687 figures).
**Companion:** `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/heldout_composite_check.py` (aggregate-only gate; same generation path).

**What it reads.** Both call helpers from `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/preflight_entropy_diag.py`:
- `_sample_prompts(data_dir, split, n_prompts, seed)` — `preflight_entropy_diag.py:37`. Default `--data-dir data/output/grpo/task_a`, `--split validation`, so it reads `{data_dir}/{split}.jsonl` = `data/output/grpo/task_a/validation.jsonl`. It slices conversations with the production slicer `_load_grpo_jsonl` (one row per user→assistant turn) and **dedupes by first-user-message** (`preflight_entropy_diag.py:60-77`). That dedupe is why `--n-prompts 290` yields **284 unique rows** (per doc §11); the earlier run used `--n-prompts 150`.
- `_decode_gt(gt_str)` — `preflight_entropy_diag.py:88` — decodes the per-row gold JSON (`state_sequence`, `tool_calls`, `terminal_state`).

**How state accuracy is computed.** `heldout_composite_audit.py::_components` (`heldout_composite_audit.py:43-92`):
- `pred_trans = extract_state_annotations(comp)` (parses `[STATE: X → Y]` from the completion),
- `gt_trans` built from `gt["state_sequence"]` `{from,to}` pairs,
- `state_acc = state_sequence_match(pred_trans, gt_trans)` when GT has a transition, else `1.0 if not pred_trans else 0.0` (`heldout_composite_audit.py:64-68`).
- Composite = `0.4*state_acc + 0.4*tool_f1 + 0.2*task` (`heldout_composite_audit.py:77`).

**GPU requirement — this is the crux for your comparison.** The audit has two separable phases:
- **Generation (needs GPU):** `_generate_for_checkpoint` (`preflight_entropy_diag.py:142`) imports `unsloth.FastLanguageModel`, loads the checkpoint `load_in_4bit=True`, and greedily decodes (`heldout_composite_audit.py:118-130`). This is the only GPU-bound part.
- **Scoring (pure CPU):** `_components` and the whole reduction (`heldout_composite_audit.py:132-158`) are pure Python. `_heldout_composite_score` in `grpo.py:541` is explicitly documented "Pure/CPU-only and unit-tested."

So: **if per-row completions were cached, re-scoring / re-slicing state accuracy needs no GPU.** They are not cached (see §4). Regenerating completions on a checkpoint requires a GPU.

## 2. State-accuracy eval logic — `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/state_accuracy.py`

- Regex `_STATE_PATTERN` (`state_accuracy.py:19-21`): matches `[STATE: X → Y]` or `[STATE: X -> Y]`.
- `parse_state_transitions(messages)` (`state_accuracy.py:64`): pulls `(from,to)` tuples from assistant messages in order.
- `compute_transition_accuracy(predicted, ground_truth)` (`state_accuracy.py:103`): **positional per-turn** scoring — `correct` when `predicted[i] == ground_truth[i]`; `accuracy = correct/len(ground_truth)`; empty GT → `1.0 if not predicted else 0.0`. This is what the audit uses (via `reward_utils.state_sequence_match`, `reward_utils.py:32-40`).
- `compute_sequence_accuracy` (`state_accuracy.py:143`): alternative conversation-level LCS-recall metric (not the one driving the 0.817/0.687 numbers).
- The extraction wrappers used by the audit live in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/training/reward_utils.py:17` (`extract_state_annotations`), `:32` (`state_sequence_match`), `:43` (`tool_call_f1`), `:104` (`reached_terminal`).

Per doc §11.4 (`grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md:855-864`): `state_acc` is strictly binary (89 zeros / 195 ones at n=284); 87/89 failures get `from` right and `to` wrong — a destination-selection problem, not a formatting one.

## 3. Backup directories and current canonical data

**Three `*.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z` dirs** (all snapshot Jul 7, pre-regeneration):
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/grpo/task_a.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z/` — `train.jsonl` (75MB), `validation.jsonl` (8.6MB)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/sft/task_a_splits.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z/` — `train/validation/test.jsonl` (190MB / 22.9MB / 10.8MB)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/sft/task_a_cleaned.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z/`

**Current canonical (regenerated Jul 14):**
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/grpo/task_a/` — `train.jsonl` (66MB), `validation.jsonl` (7.6MB)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/sft/task_a_splits/` — `train/validation/test.jsonl` (93MB / 11.1MB / 5.5MB)

Note the regenerated splits are markedly smaller (e.g. splits validation 11.1MB vs 22.9MB; grpo validation 7.6MB vs 8.6MB) — consistent with the R12 malformed-role strip (commit `6114866 fix(data): strip malformed-role messages`). Both `grpo/task_a` and `sft/task_a` are DVC stage outputs and git-ignored via `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/.gitignore`.

## 4. Cached generation output from the n=284 run — NONE present

The audit was configured to write `runs/preflight/heldout_composite_audit_ckpt1000_regen.json` (doc §11, `grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md:798`), which would contain per-row `completion`, gold labels, and the three components. **That file, and every `runs/preflight/*.json` audit artifact, is gitignored and absent on disk.** `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/runs/preflight/` contains only `provenance_20260707_040507.txt`. Searches for cached per-row completions/scores (`"completion"`, `"pred_trans"`, `mean_state_acc`) across all JSON returned nothing outside `.venv`. The checkpoints dir holds only weights/`trainer_state.json`; `data/output/benchmark/` holds teacher-generated conversations and corruption reports, not model-completion audit rows.

**Conclusion:** a model-side comparison (re-scoring the n=284 completions, or scoring the same checkpoint on the backup corpus) **cannot be done without re-generating on a GPU** — no completions are saved.

## 5. Gold-distribution comparison between two data dirs — no dedicated script, but a pure-data primitive exists

There is **no existing script that diffs gold state-transition distributions between two directories.** The closest reusable, GPU-free primitive is in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/rederive_target_bar.py`:
- `build_profile(data_dir, split)` (`rederive_target_bar.py:186`) loads a dir via the CPU-only `_load_grpo_jsonl` and extracts, per row, `(has_tool_calls, has_transition, is_terminal)` from the gold `state_sequence`/`terminal_state`.
- `summarize_profile` (`rederive_target_bar.py:212`) reports `frac_tool_expected`, `frac_state_term_applies`, etc.
- CLI takes `--data-dir`/`--split` (`rederive_target_bar.py:228`), no model load.

Running it against `data/output/grpo/task_a` and against `.../task_a.pre_r12fix_backup_...` and diffing the summaries gives a pure data-side comparison of **term applicability** — but it profiles booleans, not the full `(from,to)` transition distribution. For a true transition-distribution diff you would read `gt["state_sequence"]` directly (the loader is CPU-only: `grpo.py::_load_grpo_jsonl`, `grpo.py:236`); no such script currently exists.

Caveat on the loader: `_load_grpo_jsonl` applies the GT sanitizer `_SANITIZE_INVALID_TOOL_GT` (doc §4), which drops invalid **tool_calls** on load — it does **not** touch `state_sequence`, so state-transition profiling is unaffected.

## Directly relevant doc context

The exact data-vs-model question is already posed in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md`:
- §11.5 "Open question before the retrain — Why did state accuracy fall 0.817 → 0.687?" (`:878-885`): states 0.817 is the pre-regeneration n=150 audit and 0.687 is the regenerated n=284; hypothesizes "either the R12 regeneration changed the state-transition distribution, or the earlier figure was computed on an easier slice," and recommends the cheap check: **run the same audit against the `*.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z` directories.**
- §11.6 item 1 (`:889`): "Resolve §11.5 (cheap, **no new generation for the data-side comparison**)."
- The n=150 0.817 origin: `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md:44` (state_acc 0.817 on the 109 transition-expecting rows).

**Bottom line for your sanity-check design:** the data-side half (compare gold state-transition distributions / term applicability between `grpo/task_a` and its `pre_r12fix_backup`) is fully runnable **without a GPU** today using `rederive_target_bar.py::build_profile` as a starting point (or a direct read of `state_sequence`). The model-side half (does the checkpoint behave differently on the two corpora) **requires GPU regeneration** because no per-row completions from the n=284 run were persisted.</result>

Note the regenerated splits are markedly smaller (e.g. splits validation 11.1MB vs 22.9MB; grpo validation 7.6MB vs 8.6MB) — consistent with the R12 malformed-role strip (commit `6114866 fix(data): strip malformed-role messages`). Both `grpo/task_a` and `sft/task_a` are DVC stage outputs and git-ignored via `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/data/output/.gitignore`.

## 4. Cached generation output from the n=284 run — NONE present

The audit was configured to write `runs/preflight/heldout_composite_audit_ckpt1000_regen.json` (doc §11, `grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md:798`), which would contain per-row `completion`, gold labels, and the three components. **That file, and every `runs/preflight/*.json` audit artifact, is gitignored and absent on disk.** `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/runs/preflight/` contains only `provenance_20260707_040507.txt`. Searches for cached per-row completions/scores (`"completion"`, `"pred_trans"`, `mean_state_acc`) across all JSON returned nothing outside `.venv`. The checkpoints dir holds only weights/`trainer_state.json`; `data/output/benchmark/` holds teacher-generated conversations and corruption reports, not model-completion audit rows.

**Conclusion:** a model-side comparison (re-scoring the n=284 completions, or scoring the same checkpoint on the backup corpus) **cannot be done without re-generating on a GPU** — no completions are saved.

## 5. Gold-distribution comparison between two data dirs — no dedicated script, but a pure-data primitive exists

There is **no existing script that diffs gold state-transition distributions between two directories.** The closest reusable, GPU-free primitive is in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/rederive_target_bar.py`:
- `build_profile(data_dir, split)` (`rederive_target_bar.py:186`) loads a dir via the CPU-only `_load_grpo_jsonl` and extracts, per row, `(has_tool_calls, has_transition, is_terminal)` from the gold `state_sequence`/`terminal_state`.
- `summarize_profile` (`rederive_target_bar.py:212`) reports `frac_tool_expected`, `frac_state_term_applies`, etc.
- CLI takes `--data-dir`/`--split` (`rederive_target_bar.py:228`), no model load.

Running it against `data/output/grpo/task_a` and against `.../task_a.pre_r12fix_backup_...` and diffing the summaries gives a pure data-side comparison of **term applicability** — but it profiles booleans, not the full `(from,to)` transition distribution. For a true transition-distribution diff you would read `gt["state_sequence"]` directly (the loader is CPU-only: `grpo.py::_load_grpo_jsonl`, `grpo.py:236`); no such script currently exists.

Caveat on the loader: `_load_grpo_jsonl` applies the GT sanitizer `_SANITIZE_INVALID_TOOL_GT` (doc §4), which drops invalid **tool_calls** on load — it does **not** touch `state_sequence`, so state-transition profiling is unaffected.

## Directly relevant doc context

The exact data-vs-model question is already posed in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md`:
- §11.5 "Open question before the retrain — Why did state accuracy fall 0.817 → 0.687?" (`:878-885`): states 0.817 is the pre-regeneration n=150 audit and 0.687 is the regenerated n=284; hypothesizes "either the R12 regeneration changed the state-transition distribution, or the earlier figure was computed on an easier slice," and recommends the cheap check: **run the same audit against the `*.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z` directories.**
- §11.6 item 1 (`:889`): "Resolve §11.5 (cheap, **no new generation for the data-side comparison**)."
- The n=150 0.817 origin: `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md:44` (state_acc 0.817 on the 109 transition-expecting rows).

**Bottom line for your sanity-check design:** the data-side half (compare gold state-transition distributions / term applicability between `grpo/task_a` and its `pre_r12fix_backup`) is fully runnable **without a GPU** today using `rederive_target_bar.py::build_profile` as a starting point (or a direct read of `state_sequence`). The model-side half (does the checkpoint behave differently on the two corpora) **requires GPU regeneration** because no per-row completions from the n=284 run were persisted.</result>

All three agents are back. I now have the full picture. Key synthesis before I finalize:

**Sanity-check (§11.5):** Since ckpt-1000 is *constant* across both measurements, the 0.817→0.687 drop can only come from (a) the eval data/slice changing or (b) the metric population differing between the two audit scripts — not the model. The data-side comparison (gold state-transition distributions: `data/output/grpo/task_a` vs the `.pre_r12fix_backup_...` snapshot) is **fully runnable with no GPU** via `rederive_target_bar.py::build_profile` plus a direct `state_sequence` read. Confirmatory *reproduction* (re-running the audit on the backup corpus to recover ~0.817) needs GPU — no per-row completions were cached.

No upsampling infra exists (net-new). `response_only` masking is implemented but unused. The failure is destination-selection concentrated on tool-expected rows (state acc 0.349 there vs 0.888 elsewhere).

input.content:
# Plan: Resolve §11.5, re-scope the Cat A factorial, and reconcile the doc

## Context

The Phase 2 Cat A GRPO/SFT investigation (`docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md`) just
overturned its own central diagnosis in §11. The old story — "the model announces tools but
doesn't call them" (tool-F1 = 0.087) — is stale. On the regenerated corpus (n=284) tool-F1 is
actually **0.4623**, and the *dominant* deficit is **state-transition accuracy: 0.6866 vs the
0.85 target**, specifically **destination selection** (the model gets the `from` state right and
the `to` state wrong, 87/89 failures). The pass bar is **0.80** (§10). Lever analysis (§11.3):
taking state accuracy to 0.85 alone clears the bar (0.8085 PASS); it is the cheaper lever
(+0.146 vs +0.388 on tools).

Three things must happen, in this order (§11.6):

1. **Sanity-check (§11.5):** state accuracy fell 0.817 → 0.687 across the R12 regeneration. That
   is too large for sampling noise and must be explained *before* any retrain, or the factorial
   run tunes against a moving target.
2. **Re-scope the factorial run** around state-transition (destination-selection) accuracy. The
   existing `response_only` + tool-turn-upsampling design was built for the now-retired
   tool-emission diagnosis.
3. **Clean up the doc** so a reader hits the current diagnosis, not the obsolete one.

Intended outcome: §11.5 resolved (or attributed as far as no-GPU analysis allows), a
retrain-ready experiment spec aimed at the real lever, and a doc whose top reflects §11.

**Routing (per model-routing skill):** the redesign spec (deliverable 2) is delegated to
**Opus 4.8** (user-confirmed) — high-leverage design work where a flaw wastes ~8.6h/run of H100
time, but the space is well-constrained by §11 so Fable is not warranted. Deliverables 1 and 3 are
done in this (Sonnet) session.

## Key facts established during exploration

- **The model is constant.** ckpt-1000 is the same in both the 0.817 (n=150, pre-regen) and 0.687
  (n=284, regen) measurements, so the drop can ONLY come from (a) the eval data/slice, or (b) a
  difference in the metric population/denominator between the two audit scripts. A "model got
  worse" reading is impossible. This is the model-training-eval principle: same model, different
  eval → the difference *is* the eval.
- **The two numbers may not be the same quantity.** 0.817 came from
  `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md:44` ("state_acc 0.817 on the 109 transition-expecting
  rows", n=150). 0.687 came from `scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py` (n=284, 89 zeros + 195 ones).
  Confirm both use the same scorer (`compute_transition_accuracy`, `eval/state_accuracy.py:103`,
  positional per-turn exact match) and the same "transition-expecting" population definition before
  concluding the corpus changed.
- **No completions were cached** from the n=284 run (`runs/preflight/*.json` is gitignored/absent),
  so the confirmatory reproduction needs GPU. The **data-side comparison is CPU-only.**
- **Reusable no-GPU primitives:** `_load_grpo_jsonl` (`training/grpo.py:236`, CPU-only slicer, does
  NOT touch `state_sequence`); `build_profile`/`summarize_profile` (`scripts/rederive_target_bar.py:186`,
  term applicability); `extract_state_annotations`/`state_sequence_match` (`training/reward_utils.py:17,32`).
- **Live Cat A reward ≠ the rules' description.** `training/rewards/reward_business_logic.py:42-45`
  has **4 components** (state 0.40, tool 0.40, task 0.10, transition_legality 0.10), not the
  5-component/state-0.30 in `.claude/rules/03-training.md`. A former `length_band` component was
  removed. Reward weights are **Python constants, not YAML** — re-scoping the reward means editing
  those constants.
- **No upsampling infrastructure exists** anywhere — it is net-new. `configs/training/sft_cat_a.yaml`
  is `loss_mask: "all_tokens"`, `packing: false`; `response_only` is implemented
  (`render_response_only_sample`, `sft.py:26`) but never used on Cat A.
- **Turn-type is derivable, not labeled.** State-advancing turns (`from != to`) carry NO tool calls
  ("announce-only"); self-loop turns (`from == to`) carry the tool calls. The canonical
  self-loop-aware classifier is `classify_turn` (`training/trajectory_rollout.py:170`).

## Deliverable 1 — Sanity-check §11.5 (this session, no GPU)

Write a small CPU-only analysis script (scratch or `scripts/`) that tests the three candidate
explanations for the 0.817 → 0.687 drop, in model-training-eval "data → objective → optimization →
evaluation" order:

1. **Measurement comparability (eval bug first).** Read both audit scripts
   (`scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py`, `scripts/heldout_composite_check.py`) and
   `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` §2. Confirm the 0.817 and 0.687 use the *same* scorer
   and the *same* transition-expecting population/denominator. If they differ (e.g. 0.817 was over
   "109 transition-expecting rows" but 0.687's denominator is all 284 rows), that alone may explain
   the gap and no corpus change is implicated.
2. **Corpus distribution diff (data-side).** Load gold `state_sequence` from
   `data/output/grpo/task_a/validation.jsonl` (current) vs
   `data/output/grpo/task_a.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z/validation.jsonl` via the CPU-only
   `_load_grpo_jsonl`. Compare: fraction of advancing (`from != to`) vs self-loop rows, number of
   distinct destination states, transition-type entropy, per-complexity-level (L3–L5) and
   per-domain mix, and `summarize_profile` term-applicability. A harder slice (more advancing
   transitions, more destinations, harder domains) after regeneration would make the drop a
   data-side effect, consistent with the R12 malformed-role strip shrinking the corpus.
3. **Attribution + verdict.** State which explanation(s) the evidence supports, and whether the
   drop is real difficulty change vs a measurement artifact. Flag the **GPU confirmatory
   reproduction** (re-run `heldout_composite_audit.py` on the backup corpus to recover ~0.817,
   ~23 min) as the follow-up that closes §11.5 fully on a GPU host.

Output: a written result to fold into the doc as the §11.5 resolution (Deliverable 3).

## Deliverable 2 — Re-scoped factorial spec (delegate to Opus 4.8)

Spawn an Opus subagent (`Agent`, `model: claude-opus-4-8`) to author a retrain-ready design doc at
**`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-22-cat-a-state-accuracy-factorial.md`**, given: the §11 findings,
the Deliverable 1 sanity-check result, and the exploration facts above. The spec must:

- **Reconcile the reward discrepancy** (4-component/state-0.40 live vs 5-component/state-0.30 in the
  rules) and state which baseline the redesign is measured against.
- **Target destination-selection accuracy**, not tool emission. Design the factorial cells around
  levers that plausibly move `to`-state selection — candidates to evaluate: `loss_mask:
  response_only` (concentrates gradient on the short `[STATE: X→Y]` assistant span), upsampling/
  reweighting **state-advancing turns** (`from != to`, derived via `classify_turn`), and whether the
  reward's `state_transition` weight/graded-vs-strict scoring should change. Explicitly keep or drop
  tool-turn upsampling with justification (§11.6: it may help the 53.8% zero-tool-F1 rows but does
  not address destination selection).
- **Control the confounds** the Fable review (§3) named: the GT-sanitizer change (any retrain moves
  loss recipe AND data unless controlled), and the single-turn row-slicing exposure bias (§7.1).
- **Include a power analysis:** at n≈284 validation rows with binary state_acc, what per-cell delta
  is detectable; whether the tool-expected sub-slice (n=106) is powered for a state-accuracy delta.
- **Specify success criteria** against the **0.80** bar and the state≥0.85 component target, plus the
  exact eval command (`heldout_composite_audit.py`) and artifacts to compare.
- Note that upsampling is **net-new code** and name the insertion point (`_load_grpo_jsonl`, or a new
  per-turn SFT slicer, since SFT is whole-conversation today).

This is a spec, not an implementation — building the upsampling code is a separate follow-up the
user can approve after reviewing the spec.

## Deliverable 3 — Doc cleanup (this session)

Apply targeted edits to `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` per the cleanup map:

- **Add a top-of-doc status banner** (right after the metadata block, before the TL;DR) pointing
  readers to §11 as the current, authoritative diagnosis: state-transition accuracy (0.6866 vs
  0.85), tool-F1 corrected to 0.4623, bar 0.80.
- **Rewrite the TL;DR (lines 7–16)** and **§1 (lines 18–25)** to lead with the state-transition
  diagnosis and mark the tool-emission framing as superseded by §11; keep them as dated historical
  context, not as the headline claim.
- **Line 12** is the only place asserting **0.087** as fact — annotate/correct it (superseded by
  0.4623, §11.2). Update the stale **0.75 → 0.80** and **0.674 → 0.7271** on line 9.
- **Do NOT touch line 510** (`L5 −0.087` is an unrelated per-level delta). Leave the already-corrective
  0.087 mentions (462–466, 536–542, 668, 804, 815, 819, 894) as retired-history; lightly reconcile
  only for consistency.
- **Fold in the §11.5 resolution** from Deliverable 1 and **add a pointer to the new factorial spec**
  (Deliverable 2) so §11.6's plan links to the concrete design.
- Optionally annotate the title (line 1) since the central diagnosis flipped from tools to state.

Per project convention, this is markdown — exclude it from any code commit unless the user asks to
commit docs (git-commit skill excludes markdown except README).

## Verification

- **Deliverable 1:** run the analysis script; confirm it prints the measurement-comparability check
  result and the two-corpus distribution diff with concrete numbers, and states an attribution
  verdict. Cross-check that `_load_grpo_jsonl` row counts on the current corpus reproduce §8.1's
  census (2,943 rows / 1,126 tool-expected) as a smoke test that the slicer is being driven correctly.
- **Deliverable 2:** the Opus-authored spec exists at the named path, reconciles the reward
  discrepancy, defines factorial cells + success criteria against 0.80, includes the power analysis,
  and controls the GT-sanitizer + row-slicing confounds. Read it end-to-end for internal consistency
  before accepting.
- **Deliverable 3:** re-read the doc top-to-bottom; a reader hitting the top now sees the state
  diagnosis and a pointer to §11; no surviving assertion of 0.087/0.75/0.674 as current fact; line
  510 untouched; §11.5 shows a resolution and §11.6 links the new spec.

## Out of scope (flag, don't do)

- The **GPU confirmatory reproduction** of ~0.817 on the backup corpus (needs a GPU host).
- **Implementing** the upsampling code and running the factorial retrain (~8.6h/run) — gated on
  user approval of the Deliverable 2 spec.
- `dvc commit`/`dvc push` to refresh the stale lock (no DVC CLI in this environment; noted in R12).

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/vscode/.claude/plans/and-model-training-eval-and-model-routin-elegant-lynx.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.plan:
# Plan: Resolve §11.5, re-scope the Cat A factorial, and reconcile the doc

## Context

The Phase 2 Cat A GRPO/SFT investigation (`docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md`) just
overturned its own central diagnosis in §11. The old story — "the model announces tools but
doesn't call them" (tool-F1 = 0.087) — is stale. On the regenerated corpus (n=284) tool-F1 is
actually **0.4623**, and the *dominant* deficit is **state-transition accuracy: 0.6866 vs the
0.85 target**, specifically **destination selection** (the model gets the `from` state right and
the `to` state wrong, 87/89 failures). The pass bar is **0.80** (§10). Lever analysis (§11.3):
taking state accuracy to 0.85 alone clears the bar (0.8085 PASS); it is the cheaper lever
(+0.146 vs +0.388 on tools).

Three things must happen, in this order (§11.6):

1. **Sanity-check (§11.5):** state accuracy fell 0.817 → 0.687 across the R12 regeneration. That
   is too large for sampling noise and must be explained *before* any retrain, or the factorial
   run tunes against a moving target.
2. **Re-scope the factorial run** around state-transition (destination-selection) accuracy. The
   existing `response_only` + tool-turn-upsampling design was built for the now-retired
   tool-emission diagnosis.
3. **Clean up the doc** so a reader hits the current diagnosis, not the obsolete one.

Intended outcome: §11.5 resolved (or attributed as far as no-GPU analysis allows), a
retrain-ready experiment spec aimed at the real lever, and a doc whose top reflects §11.

**Routing (per model-routing skill):** the redesign spec (deliverable 2) is delegated to
**Opus 4.8** (user-confirmed) — high-leverage design work where a flaw wastes ~8.6h/run of H100
time, but the space is well-constrained by §11 so Fable is not warranted. Deliverables 1 and 3 are
done in this (Sonnet) session.

## Key facts established during exploration

- **The model is constant.** ckpt-1000 is the same in both the 0.817 (n=150, pre-regen) and 0.687
  (n=284, regen) measurements, so the drop can ONLY come from (a) the eval data/slice, or (b) a
  difference in the metric population/denominator between the two audit scripts. A "model got
  worse" reading is impossible. This is the model-training-eval principle: same model, different
  eval → the difference *is* the eval.
- **The two numbers may not be the same quantity.** 0.817 came from
  `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md:44` ("state_acc 0.817 on the 109 transition-expecting
  rows", n=150). 0.687 came from `scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py` (n=284, 89 zeros + 195 ones).
  Confirm both use the same scorer (`compute_transition_accuracy`, `eval/state_accuracy.py:103`,
  positional per-turn exact match) and the same "transition-expecting" population definition before
  concluding the corpus changed.
- **No completions were cached** from the n=284 run (`runs/preflight/*.json` is gitignored/absent),
  so the confirmatory reproduction needs GPU. The **data-side comparison is CPU-only.**
- **Reusable no-GPU primitives:** `_load_grpo_jsonl` (`training/grpo.py:236`, CPU-only slicer, does
  NOT touch `state_sequence`); `build_profile`/`summarize_profile` (`scripts/rederive_target_bar.py:186`,
  term applicability); `extract_state_annotations`/`state_sequence_match` (`training/reward_utils.py:17,32`).
- **Live Cat A reward ≠ the rules' description.** `training/rewards/reward_business_logic.py:42-45`
  has **4 components** (state 0.40, tool 0.40, task 0.10, transition_legality 0.10), not the
  5-component/state-0.30 in `.claude/rules/03-training.md`. A former `length_band` component was
  removed. Reward weights are **Python constants, not YAML** — re-scoping the reward means editing
  those constants.
- **No upsampling infrastructure exists** anywhere — it is net-new. `configs/training/sft_cat_a.yaml`
  is `loss_mask: "all_tokens"`, `packing: false`; `response_only` is implemented
  (`render_response_only_sample`, `sft.py:26`) but never used on Cat A.
- **Turn-type is derivable, not labeled.** State-advancing turns (`from != to`) carry NO tool calls
  ("announce-only"); self-loop turns (`from == to`) carry the tool calls. The canonical
  self-loop-aware classifier is `classify_turn` (`training/trajectory_rollout.py:170`).

## Deliverable 1 — Sanity-check §11.5 (this session, no GPU)

Write a small CPU-only analysis script (scratch or `scripts/`) that tests the three candidate
explanations for the 0.817 → 0.687 drop, in model-training-eval "data → objective → optimization →
evaluation" order:

1. **Measurement comparability (eval bug first).** Read both audit scripts
   (`scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py`, `scripts/heldout_composite_check.py`) and
   `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` §2. Confirm the 0.817 and 0.687 use the *same* scorer
   and the *same* transition-expecting population/denominator. If they differ (e.g. 0.817 was over
   "109 transition-expecting rows" but 0.687's denominator is all 284 rows), that alone may explain
   the gap and no corpus change is implicated.
2. **Corpus distribution diff (data-side).** Load gold `state_sequence` from
   `data/output/grpo/task_a/validation.jsonl` (current) vs
   `data/output/grpo/task_a.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z/validation.jsonl` via the CPU-only
   `_load_grpo_jsonl`. Compare: fraction of advancing (`from != to`) vs self-loop rows, number of
   distinct destination states, transition-type entropy, per-complexity-level (L3–L5) and
   per-domain mix, and `summarize_profile` term-applicability. A harder slice (more advancing
   transitions, more destinations, harder domains) after regeneration would make the drop a
   data-side effect, consistent with the R12 malformed-role strip shrinking the corpus.
3. **Attribution + verdict.** State which explanation(s) the evidence supports, and whether the
   drop is real difficulty change vs a measurement artifact. Flag the **GPU confirmatory
   reproduction** (re-run `heldout_composite_audit.py` on the backup corpus to recover ~0.817,
   ~23 min) as the follow-up that closes §11.5 fully on a GPU host.

Output: a written result to fold into the doc as the §11.5 resolution (Deliverable 3).

## Deliverable 2 — Re-scoped factorial spec (delegate to Opus 4.8)

Spawn an Opus subagent (`Agent`, `model: claude-opus-4-8`) to author a retrain-ready design doc at

  rules) and state which baseline the redesign is measured against.

  levers that plausibly move `to`-state selection — candidates to evaluate: `loss_mask:
  response_only` (concentrates gradient on the short `[STATE: X→Y]` assistant span), upsampling/
  reweighting **state-advancing turns** (`from != to`, derived via `classify_turn`), and whether the
  reward's `state_transition` weight/graded-vs-strict scoring should change. Explicitly keep or drop
  tool-turn upsampling with justification (§11.6: it may help the 53.8% zero-tool-F1 rows but does
  not address destination selection).
- **Control the confounds** the Fable review (§3) named: the GT-sanitizer change (any retrain moves
  loss recipe AND data unless controlled), and the single-turn row-slicing exposure bias (§7.1).
- **Include a power analysis:** at n≈284 validation rows with binary state_acc, what per-cell delta
  is detectable; whether the tool-expected sub-slice (n=106) is powered for a state-accuracy delta.
- **Specify success criteria** against the **0.80** bar and the state≥0.85 component target, plus the
  exact eval command (`heldout_composite_audit.py`) and artifacts to compare.
- Note that upsampling is **net-new code** and name the insertion point (`_load_grpo_jsonl`, or a new
  per-turn SFT slicer, since SFT is whole-conversation today).

This is a spec, not an implementation — building the upsampling code is a separate follow-up the
user can approve after reviewing the spec.

## Deliverable 3 — Doc cleanup (this session)

Apply targeted edits to `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` per the cleanup map:

- **Add a top-of-doc status banner** (right after the metadata block, before the TL;DR) pointing
  readers to §11 as the current, authoritative diagnosis: state-transition accuracy (0.6866 vs
  0.85), tool-F1 corrected to 0.4623, bar 0.80.
- **Rewrite the TL;DR (lines 7–16)** and **§1 (lines 18–25)** to lead with the state-transition
  diagnosis and mark the tool-emission framing as superseded by §11; keep them as dated historical
  context, not as the headline claim.
- **Line 12** is the only place asserting **0.087** as fact — annotate/correct it (superseded by
  0.4623, §11.2). Update the stale **0.75 → 0.80** and **0.674 → 0.7271** on line 9.
- **Do NOT touch line 510** (`L5 −0.087` is an unrelated per-level delta). Leave the already-corrective
  0.087 mentions (462–466, 536–542, 668, 804, 815, 819, 894) as retired-history; lightly reconcile
  only for consistency.
- **Fold in the §11.5 resolution** from Deliverable 1 and **add a pointer to the new factorial spec**
  (Deliverable 2) so §11.6's plan links to the concrete design.

Per project convention, this is markdown — exclude it from any code commit unless the user asks to
commit docs (git-commit skill excludes markdown except README).

  result and the two-corpus distribution diff with concrete numbers, and states an attribution

- **Deliverable 2:** the Opus-authored spec exists at the named path, reconciles the reward
  discrepancy, defines factorial cells + success criteria against 0.80, includes the power analysis,
  and controls the GT-sanitizer + row-slicing confounds. Read it end-to-end for internal consistency
  before accepting.
- **Deliverable 3:** re-read the doc top-to-bottom; a reader hitting the top now sees the state
  diagnosis and a pointer to §11; no surviving assertion of 0.087/0.75/0.674 as current fact; line
  510 untouched; §11.5 shows a resolution and §11.6 links the new spec.

## Out of scope (flag, don't do)

- The **GPU confirmatory reproduction** of ~0.817 on the backup corpus (needs a GPU host).
- **Implementing** the upsampling code and running the factorial retrain (~8.6h/run) — gated on
  user approval of the Deliverable 2 spec.
- `dvc commit`/`dvc push` to refresh the stale lock (no DVC CLI in this environment; noted in R12).

toolUseResult.plan:
# Plan: Resolve §11.5, re-scope the Cat A factorial, and reconcile the doc

## Context

The Phase 2 Cat A GRPO/SFT investigation (`docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md`) just
overturned its own central diagnosis in §11. The old story — "the model announces tools but
doesn't call them" (tool-F1 = 0.087) — is stale. On the regenerated corpus (n=284) tool-F1 is
actually **0.4623**, and the *dominant* deficit is **state-transition accuracy: 0.6866 vs the
0.85 target**, specifically **destination selection** (the model gets the `from` state right and
the `to` state wrong, 87/89 failures). The pass bar is **0.80** (§10). Lever analysis (§11.3):
taking state accuracy to 0.85 alone clears the bar (0.8085 PASS); it is the cheaper lever
(+0.146 vs +0.388 on tools).

Three things must happen, in this order (§11.6):

1. **Sanity-check (§11.5):** state accuracy fell 0.817 → 0.687 across the R12 regeneration. That
   is too large for sampling noise and must be explained *before* any retrain, or the factorial
   run tunes against a moving target.
2. **Re-scope the factorial run** around state-transition (destination-selection) accuracy. The
   existing `response_only` + tool-turn-upsampling design was built for the now-retired
   tool-emission diagnosis.
3. **Clean up the doc** so a reader hits the current diagnosis, not the obsolete one.

Intended outcome: §11.5 resolved (or attributed as far as no-GPU analysis allows), a
retrain-ready experiment spec aimed at the real lever, and a doc whose top reflects §11.

**Routing (per model-routing skill):** the redesign spec (deliverable 2) is delegated to
**Opus 4.8** (user-confirmed) — high-leverage design work where a flaw wastes ~8.6h/run of H100
time, but the space is well-constrained by §11 so Fable is not warranted. Deliverables 1 and 3 are
done in this (Sonnet) session.

## Key facts established during exploration

- **The model is constant.** ckpt-1000 is the same in both the 0.817 (n=150, pre-regen) and 0.687
  (n=284, regen) measurements, so the drop can ONLY come from (a) the eval data/slice, or (b) a
  difference in the metric population/denominator between the two audit scripts. A "model got
  worse" reading is impossible. This is the model-training-eval principle: same model, different
  eval → the difference *is* the eval.
- **The two numbers may not be the same quantity.** 0.817 came from
  `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md:44` ("state_acc 0.817 on the 109 transition-expecting
  rows", n=150). 0.687 came from `scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py` (n=284, 89 zeros + 195 ones).
  Confirm both use the same scorer (`compute_transition_accuracy`, `eval/state_accuracy.py:103`,
  positional per-turn exact match) and the same "transition-expecting" population definition before
  concluding the corpus changed.
- **No completions were cached** from the n=284 run (`runs/preflight/*.json` is gitignored/absent),
  so the confirmatory reproduction needs GPU. The **data-side comparison is CPU-only.**
- **Reusable no-GPU primitives:** `_load_grpo_jsonl` (`training/grpo.py:236`, CPU-only slicer, does
  NOT touch `state_sequence`); `build_profile`/`summarize_profile` (`scripts/rederive_target_bar.py:186`,
  term applicability); `extract_state_annotations`/`state_sequence_match` (`training/reward_utils.py:17,32`).
- **Live Cat A reward ≠ the rules' description.** `training/rewards/reward_business_logic.py:42-45`
  has **4 components** (state 0.40, tool 0.40, task 0.10, transition_legality 0.10), not the
  5-component/state-0.30 in `.claude/rules/03-training.md`. A former `length_band` component was
  removed. Reward weights are **Python constants, not YAML** — re-scoping the reward means editing
  those constants.
- **No upsampling infrastructure exists** anywhere — it is net-new. `configs/training/sft_cat_a.yaml`
  is `loss_mask: "all_tokens"`, `packing: false`; `response_only` is implemented
  (`render_response_only_sample`, `sft.py:26`) but never used on Cat A.
- **Turn-type is derivable, not labeled.** State-advancing turns (`from != to`) carry NO tool calls
  ("announce-only"); self-loop turns (`from == to`) carry the tool calls. The canonical
  self-loop-aware classifier is `classify_turn` (`training/trajectory_rollout.py:170`).

## Deliverable 1 — Sanity-check §11.5 (this session, no GPU)

Write a small CPU-only analysis script (scratch or `scripts/`) that tests the three candidate
explanations for the 0.817 → 0.687 drop, in model-training-eval "data → objective → optimization →
evaluation" order:

1. **Measurement comparability (eval bug first).** Read both audit scripts
   (`scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py`, `scripts/heldout_composite_check.py`) and
   `docs/grpo_heldout_metric_and_gt_audit.md` §2. Confirm the 0.817 and 0.687 use the *same* scorer
   and the *same* transition-expecting population/denominator. If they differ (e.g. 0.817 was over
   "109 transition-expecting rows" but 0.687's denominator is all 284 rows), that alone may explain
   the gap and no corpus change is implicated.
2. **Corpus distribution diff (data-side).** Load gold `state_sequence` from
   `data/output/grpo/task_a/validation.jsonl` (current) vs
   `data/output/grpo/task_a.pre_r12fix_backup_20260714T104107Z/validation.jsonl` via the CPU-only
   `_load_grpo_jsonl`. Compare: fraction of advancing (`from != to`) vs self-loop rows, number of
   distinct destination states, transition-type entropy, per-complexity-level (L3–L5) and
   per-domain mix, and `summarize_profile` term-applicability. A harder slice (more advancing
   transitions, more destinations, harder domains) after regeneration would make the drop a
   data-side effect, consistent with the R12 malformed-role strip shrinking the corpus.
3. **Attribution + verdict.** State which explanation(s) the evidence supports, and whether the
   drop is real difficulty change vs a measurement artifact. Flag the **GPU confirmatory
   reproduction** (re-run `heldout_composite_audit.py` on the backup corpus to recover ~0.817,
   ~23 min) as the follow-up that closes §11.5 fully on a GPU host.

Output: a written result to fold into the doc as the §11.5 resolution (Deliverable 3).

## Deliverable 2 — Re-scoped factorial spec (delegate to Opus 4.8)

Spawn an Opus subagent (`Agent`, `model: claude-opus-4-8`) to author a retrain-ready design doc at
**`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-22-cat-a-state-accuracy-factorial.md`**, given: the §11 findings,
the Deliverable 1 sanity-check result, and the exploration facts above. The spec must:

- **Reconcile the reward discrepancy** (4-component/state-0.40 live vs 5-component/state-0.30 in the
  rules) and state which baseline the redesign is measured against.
- **Target destination-selection accuracy**, not tool emission. Design the factorial cells around
  levers that plausibly move `to`-state selection — candidates to evaluate: `loss_mask:
  response_only` (concentrates gradient on the short `[STATE: X→Y]` assistant span), upsampling/
  reweighting **state-advancing turns** (`from != to`, derived via `classify_turn`), and whether the
  reward's `state_transition` weight/graded-vs-strict scoring should change. Explicitly keep or drop
  tool-turn upsampling with justification (§11.6: it may help the 53.8% zero-tool-F1 rows but does
  not address destination selection).
- **Control the confounds** the Fable review (§3) named: the GT-sanitizer change (any retrain moves
  loss recipe AND data unless controlled), and the single-turn row-slicing exposure bias (§7.1).
- **Include a power analysis:** at n≈284 validation rows with binary state_acc, what per-cell delta
  is detectable; whether the tool-expected sub-slice (n=106) is powered for a state-accuracy delta.
- **Specify success criteria** against the **0.80** bar and the state≥0.85 component target, plus the
  exact eval command (`heldout_composite_audit.py`) and artifacts to compare.
- Note that upsampling is **net-new code** and name the insertion point (`_load_grpo_jsonl`, or a new
  per-turn SFT slicer, since SFT is whole-conversation today).

This is a spec, not an implementation — building the upsampling code is a separate follow-up the
user can approve after reviewing the spec.

## Deliverable 3 — Doc cleanup (this session)

Apply targeted edits to `docs/grpo_tool_emission_gap_review.md` per the cleanup map:

- **Add a top-of-doc status banner** (right after the metadata block, before the TL;DR) pointing
  readers to §11 as the current, authoritative diagnosis: state-transition accuracy (0.6866 vs
  0.85), tool-F1 corrected to 0.4623, bar 0.80.
- **Rewrite the TL;DR (lines 7–16)** and **§1 (lines 18–25)** to lead with the state-transition
  diagnosis and mark the tool-emission framing as superseded by §11; keep them as dated historical
  context, not as the headline claim.
- **Line 12** is the only place asserting **0.087** as fact — annotate/correct it (superseded by
  0.4623, §11.2). Update the stale **0.75 → 0.80** and **0.674 → 0.7271** on line 9.
- **Do NOT touch line 510** (`L5 −0.087` is an unrelated per-level delta). Leave the already-corrective
  0.087 mentions (462–466, 536–542, 668, 804, 815, 819, 894) as retired-history; lightly reconcile
  only for consistency.
- **Fold in the §11.5 resolution** from Deliverable 1 and **add a pointer to the new factorial spec**
  (Deliverable 2) so §11.6's plan links to the concrete design.
- Optionally annotate the title (line 1) since the central diagnosis flipped from tools to state.

Per project convention, this is markdown — exclude it from any code commit unless the user asks to
commit docs (git-commit skill excludes markdown except README).

## Verification

- **Deliverable 1:** run the analysis script; confirm it prints the measurement-comparability check
  result and the two-corpus distribution diff with concrete numbers, and states an attribution
  verdict. Cross-check that `_load_grpo_jsonl` row counts on the current corpus reproduce §8.1's
  census (2,943 rows / 1,126 tool-expected) as a smoke test that the slicer is being driven correctly.
- **Deliverable 2:** the Opus-authored spec exists at the named path, reconciles the reward
  discrepancy, defines factorial cells + success criteria against 0.80, includes the power analysis,
  and controls the GT-sanitizer + row-slicing confounds. Read it end-to-end for internal consistency
  before accepting.
- **Deliverable 3:** re-read the doc top-to-bottom; a reader hitting the top now sees the state
  diagnosis and a pointer to §11; no surviving assertion of 0.087/0.75/0.674 as current fact; line
  510 untouched; §11.5 shows a resolution and §11.6 links the new spec.

## Out of scope (flag, don't do)

- The **GPU confirmatory reproduction** of ~0.817 on the backup corpus (needs a GPU host).
- **Implementing** the upsampling code and running the factorial retrain (~8.6h/run) — gated on
  user approval of the Deliverable 2 spec.
- `dvc commit`/`dvc push` to refresh the stale lock (no DVC CLI in this environment; noted in R12).

3	
4	The ceiling check reports only the aggregate composite + per-row scalar. When
5	it FAILs (mean < 0.75) the action is to audit reward/GT on the low rows before
6	assuming a hard policy ceiling. This script re-runs the identical greedy pass
7	(same sampler, seed, checkpoint -> identical completions) but persists, per row:
8	
9	  - the model completion text,
10	  - the GT state_sequence / tool_calls / terminal_state,
11	  - the THREE strict composite components separately
12	    (state_acc, tool_f1, task) so we can see WHICH term is dragging the
13	    composite down, and whether it's a genuine policy miss or a reward/GT
14	    artifact (e.g. GT tool_calls with placeholder args the model can't match).
15	
16	Component math mirrors grpo._heldout_composite_score exactly:
17	  composite = 0.4*state_acc + 0.4*tool_f1 + 0.2*task
18	
19	Usage:
20	    .venv-train/bin/python scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py \
21	        --checkpoint checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000 \

24	"""

27

29	import json

44	    """Strict composite components for one row — same scorers as

46	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.reward_utils import (
47	        extract_state_annotations,

52	    )

99	    parser.add_argument("--split", default="validation")

113	    gts = [_decode_gt(p["ground_truth"]) for p in prompts]
114	    print(f"[data] sampled {len(prompts)} prompts", flush=True)
115	
116	    t0 = time.time()
117	    print("[gen] greedy pass (do_sample=False)", flush=True)
118	    greedy = _generate_for_checkpoint(

127	        batch_size=args.batch_size,
128	        seed=args.seed,
129	    )
130	    completions = [c[0] if c else "" for c in greedy]
131	
132	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
133	    for i, (comp, gt) in enumerate(zip(completions, gts, strict=True)):
134	        comps = _components(comp, gt)
135	        rows.append({"row_index": i, "completion": comp, **comps})
136	
137	    rows_sorted = sorted(rows, key=lambda r: r["composite"])
138	    n = len(rows)
139	
140	    def _mean(key: str) -> float:
141	        return sum(r[key] for r in rows) / n if n else 0.0
142	
143	    summary = {
144	        "n_rows": n,
145	        "mean_composite": _mean("composite"),
146	        "mean_state_acc": _mean("state_acc"),
147	        "mean_tool_f1": _mean("tool_f1"),
148	        "mean_task": _mean("task"),
149	        "frac_state_acc_zero": sum(1 for r in rows if r["state_acc"] == 0.0) / n,
150	        "frac_tool_f1_zero": sum(1 for r in rows if r["tool_f1"] == 0.0) / n,
151	        "frac_task_zero": sum(1 for r in rows if r["task"] == 0.0) / n,
152	        "frac_gt_has_no_tools": sum(1 for r in rows if r["n_gt_tools"] == 0) / n,
153	        "wall_time_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
154	    }
155	
156	    args.output.write_text(
157	        json.dumps({"summary": summary, "rows": rows_sorted}, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
158	    )
159	
160	    print(
161	        "\n=== held-out composite AUDIT — component means ===\n"
162	        f"  mean_composite = {summary['mean_composite']:.4f}\n"
163	        f"  mean_state_acc = {summary['mean_state_acc']:.4f}  (weight 0.4)\n"
164	        f"  mean_tool_f1   = {summary['mean_tool_f1']:.4f}  (weight 0.4)\n"
165	        f"  mean_task      = {summary['mean_task']:.4f}  (weight 0.2)\n"
166	        f"  frac state_acc==0 : {summary['frac_state_acc_zero']:.3f}\n"
167	        f"  frac tool_f1==0   : {summary['frac_tool_f1_zero']:.3f}\n"
168	        f"  frac task==0      : {summary['frac_task_zero']:.3f}\n"
169	        f"  frac GT has 0 tools: {summary['frac_gt_has_no_tools']:.3f}\n"
170	        f"  wall_time_s = {summary['wall_time_s']}\n"
171	        f"[done] wrote {args.output}",
172	        flush=True,
173	    )
174	    return 0
175	
176	
177	if __name__ == "__main__":
178	    raise SystemExit(main())

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Row-level audit companion to scripts/heldout_composite_check.py.

The ceiling check reports only the aggregate composite + per-row scalar. When
it FAILs (mean < 0.75) the action is to audit reward/GT on the low rows before
assuming a hard policy ceiling. This script re-runs the identical greedy pass
(same sampler, seed, checkpoint -> identical completions) but persists, per row:

  - the model completion text,
  - the GT state_sequence / tool_calls / terminal_state,
  - the THREE strict composite components separately
    (state_acc, tool_f1, task) so we can see WHICH term is dragging the
    composite down, and whether it's a genuine policy miss or a reward/GT
    artifact (e.g. GT tool_calls with placeholder args the model can't match).

Component math mirrors grpo._heldout_composite_score exactly:
  composite = 0.4*state_acc + 0.4*tool_f1 + 0.2*task

Usage:
    .venv-train/bin/python scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py \
        --checkpoint checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000 \
        --data-dir data/output/grpo/task_a --split validation \
        --n-prompts 150 --output runs/preflight/heldout_composite_audit_ckpt1000.json
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
SRC = REPO_ROOT / "src"
if str(SRC) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC))
if str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))


def _components(comp: str, gt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Strict composite components for one row — same scorers as
    grpo._heldout_composite_score, broken out instead of summed."""
    from llm_workflow_agents.training.reward_utils import (
        extract_state_annotations,
        extract_tool_calls,
        reached_terminal,
        state_sequence_match,
        tool_call_f1,
    )

    gt = gt or {}
    gt_seq = gt.get("state_sequence") or []
    gt_trans = [
        (s.get("from", ""), s.get("to", ""))
        if isinstance(s, dict)
        else tuple(s)
        if isinstance(s, (list, tuple)) and len(s) == 2
        else ("", "")
        for s in gt_seq
    ]
    pred_trans = extract_state_annotations(comp)
    if gt_trans:
        state_acc = state_sequence_match(pred_trans, gt_trans)
    else:
        state_acc = 1.0 if not pred_trans else 0.0

    gt_tools = gt.get("tool_calls") or []
    pred_tools = extract_tool_calls(comp)
    tool_f1 = tool_call_f1(pred_tools, gt_tools)

    terminal = gt.get("terminal_state") or ""
    task = 1.0 if terminal and reached_terminal(comp, terminal) else 0.0

    composite = 0.4 * state_acc + 0.4 * tool_f1 + 0.2 * task
    return {
        "composite": composite,
        "state_acc": state_acc,
        "tool_f1": tool_f1,
        "task": task,
        "n_gt_trans": len(gt_trans),
        "n_pred_trans": len(pred_trans),
        "n_gt_tools": len(gt_tools),
        "n_pred_tools": len(pred_tools),
        "gt_state_sequence": gt_seq,
        "gt_tool_calls": gt_tools,
        "gt_terminal": terminal,
        "pred_trans": pred_trans,
        "pred_tools": pred_tools,
    }


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--data-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/output/grpo/task_a"))
    parser.add_argument("--split", default="validation")
    parser.add_argument("--n-prompts", type=int, default=150)
    parser.add_argument("--max-new-tokens", type=int, default=512)
    parser.add_argument("--max-seq-length", type=int, default=8192)
    parser.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=4)
    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    from preflight_entropy_diag import _decode_gt, _generate_for_checkpoint, _sample_prompts

    prompts = _sample_prompts(args.data_dir, args.split, args.n_prompts, args.seed)
    gts = [_decode_gt(p["ground_truth"]) for p in prompts]
    print(f"[data] sampled {len(prompts)} prompts", flush=True)

    t0 = time.time()
    print("[gen] greedy pass (do_sample=False)", flush=True)
    greedy = _generate_for_checkpoint(
        checkpoint=args.checkpoint,
        prompts=prompts,
        n_completions=1,
        do_sample=False,
        max_new_tokens=args.max_new_tokens,
        temperature=0.0,
        top_p=1.0,
        max_seq_length=args.max_seq_length,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        seed=args.seed,
    )
    completions = [c[0] if c else "" for c in greedy]

    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for i, (comp, gt) in enumerate(zip(completions, gts, strict=True)):
        comps = _components(comp, gt)
        rows.append({"row_index": i, "completion": comp, **comps})

    rows_sorted = sorted(rows, key=lambda r: r["composite"])
    n = len(rows)

    def _mean(key: str) -> float:
        return sum(r[key] for r in rows) / n if n else 0.0

    summary = {
        "n_rows": n,
        "mean_composite": _mean("composite"),
        "mean_state_acc": _mean("state_acc"),
        "mean_tool_f1": _mean("tool_f1"),
        "mean_task": _mean("task"),
        "frac_state_acc_zero": sum(1 for r in rows if r["state_acc"] == 0.0) / n,
        "frac_tool_f1_zero": sum(1 for r in rows if r["tool_f1"] == 0.0) / n,
        "frac_task_zero": sum(1 for r in rows if r["task"] == 0.0) / n,
        "frac_gt_has_no_tools": sum(1 for r in rows if r["n_gt_tools"] == 0) / n,
        "wall_time_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
    }

    args.output.write_text(
        json.dumps({"summary": summary, "rows": rows_sorted}, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    )

    print(
        "\n=== held-out composite AUDIT — component means ===\n"
        f"  mean_composite = {summary['mean_composite']:.4f}\n"
        f"  mean_state_acc = {summary['mean_state_acc']:.4f}  (weight 0.4)\n"
        f"  mean_tool_f1   = {summary['mean_tool_f1']:.4f}  (weight 0.4)\n"
        f"  mean_task      = {summary['mean_task']:.4f}  (weight 0.2)\n"
        f"  frac state_acc==0 : {summary['frac_state_acc_zero']:.3f}\n"
        f"  frac tool_f1==0   : {summary['frac_tool_f1_zero']:.3f}\n"
        f"  frac task==0      : {summary['frac_task_zero']:.3f}\n"
        f"  frac GT has 0 tools: {summary['frac_gt_has_no_tools']:.3f}\n"
        f"  wall_time_s = {summary['wall_time_s']}\n"
        f"[done] wrote {args.output}",
        flush=True,
    )
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""SFT-only ceiling check — greedy held-out composite vs the Phase 2 target.

Answers the fork documented in docs/grpo_viability_investigation.md §5.2: if
both the RFT headroom probe and the trajectory variance probe land short of
GO, does the SFT checkpoint's greedy held-out composite already clear the
Phase 2 target on its own? If it does, SFT-only is the correct terminal
state (RL has nothing left to add) rather than a compromise. If it doesn't,
the gap without measurable RL headroom points at a reward/ground-truth issue
to audit (e.g. the placeholder-arg tool-call stubs) before concluding the
policy is capped.

Unlike the RFT/trajectory probes this does one greedy pass only (no
sampling, no headroom) and scores with the *strict* composite
(``_heldout_composite_score`` — the same metric ``_HeldOutEvalCallback``
logs as ``eval/held_out_composite`` during live GRPO training), not the
graded training reward. Target: >= 0.75 (eval/composite_score.py,
``compute_weighted_workflow_score``).

Gate:
  PASS : mean_composite >= 0.75  -> ship SFT-only.
  FAIL : mean_composite <  0.75  -> audit reward/GT before assuming a ceiling.

The reduction (summarize_heldout_check) and the gate (classify_gate) are
pure and unit-tested in tests/unit/test_heldout_composite_check.py.

Usage:
    .venv-train/bin/python scripts/heldout_composite_check.py \
        --checkpoint checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000 \
        --data-dir data/output/grpo/task_a --split validation \
        --n-prompts 150 --output runs/preflight/heldout_composite_ckpt1000.json
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import statistics
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
SRC = REPO_ROOT / "src"
if str(SRC) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC))
if str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))

# Gate threshold (eval/composite_score.py: compute_weighted_workflow_score target)
TARGET_COMPOSITE = 0.75


def summarize_heldout_check(row_scores: list[float]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Reduce per-row composite scores to the probe summary.

    Pure — no model, no I/O. ``row_scores[i]`` is the strict held-out
    composite for held-out row i (one greedy completion each).
    """
    per_row: list[dict[str, Any]] = [
        {"row_index": i, "composite": s} for i, s in enumerate(row_scores)
    ]

    n = len(row_scores)
    return {
        "n_rows": n,
        "mean_composite": statistics.fmean(row_scores) if row_scores else 0.0,
        "median_composite": statistics.median(row_scores) if row_scores else 0.0,
        "min_composite": min(row_scores) if row_scores else 0.0,
        "max_composite": max(row_scores) if row_scores else 0.0,
        "frac_below_target": (
            sum(1 for s in row_scores if s < TARGET_COMPOSITE) / n if n else 0.0
        ),
        "per_row": per_row,
    }


def classify_gate(summary: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    """Classify the probe summary into a gate verdict. Pure."""
    mean_composite = summary["mean_composite"]
    passed = mean_composite >= TARGET_COMPOSITE

    verdict = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
    return verdict, {
        "checks": {
            f"mean_composite >= {TARGET_COMPOSITE}": passed,
        },
    }


_VERDICT_ACTION = {
    "PASS": (
        "SFT-only already clears the Phase 2 target — RL has nothing to add; "
        "ship SFT-only for Cat A."
    ),
    "FAIL": (
        "Below target despite no measurable RL headroom — audit reward/GT "
        "(e.g. placeholder-arg tool-call stubs) on ~50 rows before assuming "
        "a hard ceiling."
    ),
}


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True, help="SFT checkpoint to probe.")
    parser.add_argument("--data-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/output/grpo/task_a"))
    parser.add_argument("--split", default="validation")
    parser.add_argument("--n-prompts", type=int, default=150)
    parser.add_argument("--max-new-tokens", type=int, default=512)
    parser.add_argument("--max-seq-length", type=int, default=8192)
    parser.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=4)
    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    from preflight_entropy_diag import _decode_gt, _generate_for_checkpoint, _sample_prompts

    from llm_workflow_agents.training.grpo import _heldout_composite_score

    prompts = _sample_prompts(args.data_dir, args.split, args.n_prompts, args.seed)
    gts = [_decode_gt(p["ground_truth"]) for p in prompts]
    print(
        f"[data] sampled {len(prompts)} prompts from {args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
        flush=True,
    )

    t0 = time.time()
    print("[gen] greedy pass (do_sample=False)", flush=True)
    greedy = _generate_for_checkpoint(
        checkpoint=args.checkpoint,
        prompts=prompts,
        n_completions=1,
        do_sample=False,
        max_new_tokens=args.max_new_tokens,
        temperature=0.0,
        top_p=1.0,
        max_seq_length=args.max_seq_length,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        seed=args.seed,
    )
    completions = [c[0] if c else "" for c in greedy]

    row_scores = [
        _heldout_composite_score([comp], [gt])
        for comp, gt in zip(completions, gts, strict=True)
    ]

    summary = summarize_heldout_check(row_scores)
    summary["wall_time_s"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
    verdict, detail = classify_gate(summary)

    results = {
        "config": {
            "checkpoint": args.checkpoint,
            "data": f"{args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
            "n_prompts": summary["n_rows"],
            "seed": args.seed,
            "target_composite": TARGET_COMPOSITE,
        },
        "summary": {k: v for k, v in summary.items() if k != "per_row"},
        "gate": {"verdict": verdict, **detail, "action": _VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]},
        "per_row": summary["per_row"],
    }
    args.output.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))

    print(
        f"\n=== held-out composite check — {Path(args.checkpoint).name} ===\n"
        f"  mean_composite   = {summary['mean_composite']:.4f}  (PASS >= {TARGET_COMPOSITE})\n"
        f"  median_composite = {summary['median_composite']:.4f}\n"
        f"  min/max          = {summary['min_composite']:.4f} / {summary['max_composite']:.4f}\n"
        f"  frac_below_target= {summary['frac_below_target']:.3f}\n"
        f"  wall_time_s      = {summary['wall_time_s']}\n"
        f"\n  VERDICT: {verdict} — {_VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]}\n"
        f"[done] wrote {args.output}",
        flush=True,
    )
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

content.content:
1	# Held-Out Metric Fix + GT Over-Eager Audit — Cat A ckpt-1000
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-07-13
4	**Base:** `checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000` (Gemma-4 26B-A4B-it, HF-generate path per R9)
5	**Companion to:** [`grpo_viability_investigation.md`](grpo_viability_investigation.md) — closes the §5.2 fork ("ship SFT-only vs escalate") and confirms §5-item-3 (GT/reward misspecification).
6	
7	## TL;DR
8	
9	Ran the SFT-only ceiling check (`scripts/heldout_composite_check.py`). It **FAILed** at
10	**0.479** (target 0.75). A row-level audit shows the FAIL is **mostly a scoring-harness
11	artifact, not a policy ceiling**: two of the three composite terms cannot be earned at
12	the per-turn granularity GRPO rows use. Corrected to **per-turn-fair**, the honest
13	baseline is **0.674** — still below 0.75, and the residual gap is **one genuine,
14	addressable weakness** (the policy transitions state and narrates intent correctly but
15	under-emits the actual `<tool_call>`). Two fixes landed in `training/grpo.py`:
16	
17	1. **Per-turn-fair held-out metric** (`_heldout_composite_score`) — 0.479 → 0.674.
18	2. **Schema-aware GT sanitizer** — drops 32 fabricated-required-arg tool calls from the
19	   reward target so GRPO can't be trained to fabricate-and-fire.
20	
21	Neither branch of the §5.2 fork as originally framed is correct: **not** a hard ceiling
22	(state 0.82 / abstention 0.80 are strong), and **not** "SFT already clears 0.75."
23	
24	## 1. SFT-only ceiling check (as-scored)
25	
26	`scripts/heldout_composite_check.py`, ckpt-1000, 150 validation prompts, greedy, seed 42, ~14 min.
27	
28	| Metric | Value | Gate |
29	|---|---|---|
30	| mean_composite | **0.4773** | ≥ 0.75 → **FAIL** |
31	| median_composite | 0.4000 | |
32	| frac_below_target | 0.733 (110/150) | |
33	
34	Artifact: `runs/preflight/heldout_composite_ckpt1000.json` (+ `.log`).
35	
36	## 2. Row-level audit — why 0.48
37	
38	`scripts/heldout_composite_audit.py` regenerates the same greedy pass but persists every
39	completion + a per-component breakdown. It reproduces the composite (0.4787 vs 0.4773; the
40	delta is batch-padding numerics) and decomposes it:
41	
42	| Term | Weight | Mean | What's happening |
43	|---|---|---|---|
44	| **state_acc** | 0.4 | 0.60 | **0.817** where GT expects a transition (109 rows) — strong. **0.024** on the 41 empty-GT-transition turns: 40/41 the model emits its trained `[STATE:…]` annotation when GT says "no transition" → strict scorer zeroes it. **Artifact.** |
45	| **tool_f1** | 0.4 | 0.55 | **0.796** where GT expects no tool (correct abstention). **0.087** where GT expects a tool (52 rows) — **genuine weakness.** |
46	| **task** | 0.2 | **0.093** | 90.7% score 0. "Reached terminal" is a whole-conversation check; a single intermediate turn can't satisfy it (only 8/150 rows are terminal turns), and 21 rows carry an empty `terminal_state`. **Artifact.** |
47	
48	**Root cause:** the 0.75 target is `eval/composite_score.compute_weighted_workflow_score`,
49	designed as a **whole-conversation** metric. The held-out probe (and the live
50	`_HeldOutEvalCallback`) apply it to **single user→assistant turns**. The GT itself is sound
51	and correctly per-turn (median state_sequence length 1); the mismatch is the scoring
52	granularity.
53	
54	Artifact: `runs/preflight/heldout_composite_audit_ckpt1000.json` (150 completions + components).
55	
56	### The disproven hypothesis
57	The check's FAIL action pointed at "placeholder-arg tool-call stubs." That is **not** the
58	cause: all 53 GT tool calls carry concrete args (`TXN-48219`, `disputed_amount: 84.5`), zero
59	placeholders. The real depressants are the two granularity artifacts above + the tool-emission gap.
60	
61	### The one genuine weakness: "announce-but-don't-call"
62	Of the 52 rows where a tool is expected, 47 scored 0. Of the 30 that emitted **no** tool:
63	~15 are **announce-but-don't-call** (correct state transition, correct intent narration,
64	required args present, but no `<tool_call>` — e.g. gives `ORDER-55221`, transitions to
65	`LOOKUP_ORDER`, chats, never calls `lookup_order`); ~3–4 are **GT over-eager** (model
66	correctly withholds — see §4); ~6 borderline verify-first; ~2–3 degenerate (leaks
67	schema/instruction text, rare). This is a real RL/data target, not an artifact.
68	
69	## 3. Fix A — per-turn-fair held-out metric (applied)
70
